description: Japanese pan-fried dumplings with thin wrappers and a savory filling, often pork with cabbage, garlic, ginger, and chives. They are usually browned on one side and finished by steaming in the pan.
preparation_steps: Prepare the filling and place a small amount in each wrapper.
Pan-fry until the bottoms are lightly browned.
Add a small amount of water, cover, and steam until cooked through.

nutrition.fat: Low to moderate, higher if heavily fried

health_advice.benefits: Can provide a satisfying portion-controlled snack or meal, especially with a vegetable-rich filling.
health_advice.risks: Sodium can be high because of soy-based dipping sauces, and pan-frying increases oil content compared with boiling or steaming.
health_advice.healthy_tip: Use more vegetables in the filling and go easy on the dipping sauce.

dietary_notes.note: Standard wrappers usually contain wheat. Vegan versions are possible when the filling excludes meat and egg.

allergens: Shellfish if shrimp filling is used
